personal.preferred_title: Staff Data Software Engineer | AWS Python Data Platform & API Development

summary: Senior data engineer and software engineer with 20+ years of enterprise experience across Python, SQL, AWS data platforms, backend services, telemetry pipelines, and regulated financial-services environments. Strong fit for AWS/Python/API-heavy data platform work, with hands-on production experience building ETL pipelines, operational data products, forecasting systems, and reporting layers. Currently expanding Databricks, Delta Lake, DLT, Unity Catalog, and lakehouse architecture skills through self-study and local project work; CMS/healthcare domain experience should be treated as transferable regulated-enterprise experience, not direct CMS production ownership.

experience.title: Senior Capacity & Data Engineer
bullets: Architected Python/Pandas ETL pipelines ingesting P95 telemetry from 6,000+ endpoints, replacing manual operational processes with repeatable data workflows for capacity forecasting and executive reporting.
Built a hybrid AWS/Oracle data platform using S3 landing zones, AWS Glue ETL, Amazon Redshift, and Oracle reporting schemas to support large-scale telemetry processing and forecasting workloads.
Containerized Python ETL jobs on EC2/ECS and designed modular processing flows that supported scalable on-demand execution for infrastructure capacity analysis.
Designed optimized database schemas, historical retention structures, and reporting datasets used for seasonal risk analysis, operational visibility, and infrastructure planning.
Developed forecasting models using Prophet and scikit-learn to predict capacity bottlenecks months ahead, improving planning lead time and reducing dependency on spreadsheet-driven analysis.
Integrated monitoring and capacity data from BMC TrueSight, CA Wily, and AppDynamics into unified reporting layers, dashboards, and stakeholder-facing analytics.
Partnered with engineering, operations, and application teams in a regulated banking environment where data quality, access controls, documentation, and auditability were required parts of delivery.

experience.title: Performance Engineer
bullets: Managed Dynatrace AppMon and Gomez Synthetic Monitoring for Brand.com and critical hospitality systems, combining application performance telemetry with actionable engineering recommendations.
Led the FAST project, mining real-user and synthetic performance data to identify optimization opportunities adopted by engineering teams.
Upgraded Dynatrace from 6.5 to 7.0 and enforced TLS 1.2 security with no production monitoring regression during the cutover.
Implemented End-User Monitoring and transaction tracing across web and mobile flows, giving teams better visibility into customer-facing performance issues.

experience.title: SME for CA APM / Senior Consultant
bullets: Served as CA APM SME for a large financial-services client, managing 50+ Enterprise Managers and 4,000–6,000 instrumented agents across the enterprise.
Designed dashboards, alerts, SLA thresholds, and custom Management Modules that became standards across client environments.
Built Perl and Korn Shell data-extraction scripts to automate performance reporting and reduce manual weekly exports.
Collaborated with J2EE, WebLogic, WebSphere, Oracle, and application teams to diagnose performance bottlenecks using APM telemetry and operational evidence.

experience.title: Senior Systems & Data Migration Engineer
bullets: Led migration of a high-throughput shopping engine from 200+ MySQL nodes to a 6-node Oracle RAC cluster, supporting significantly higher transaction scale with a smaller footprint.
Optimized transaction processing using C++ and OCCI while maintaining sub-second query behavior for high-volume operational workloads.
Built C++/OCCI/OCI validation tooling to confirm migration correctness across large transaction datasets.
Automated schema conversion and data validation workflows to protect integrity during phased cutover.

experience.title: Developer / Support Engineer
bullets: Developed high-availability multithreaded C++ billing interfaces using POSIX threads, sockets, Pro*C, PL/SQL, and Oracle for telecom-scale transaction processing.
Delivered performance improvements in C/C++/Pro*C/PL/SQL billing processes, including memory reduction, throughput improvement, and database sequence optimization.
Maintained and enhanced billing, reporting, settlement, and interface components across full production support and delivery cycles.
Automated WebLogic/WebSphere administration and deployment tasks with Korn Shell scripting, reducing manual operational steps.

education.degree: Post-Graduate Diploma in Computer Engineering Technology / Computer Science
Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering

skills.Languages: Python, SQL, PySpark, PL/SQL, C++, Java, Perl, Korn Shell, XML, JSON

projects.name: AI-Powered Job Search Pipeline
projects.description: Built an end-to-end agentic job application pipeline using Python, Claude Code, Grok API, FAISS, sentence-transformers, JSON artifacts, quality gates, and application tracking. Demonstrates backend-style orchestration, structured data contracts, semantic duplicate detection, and LLM-assisted workflow automation.

projects.name: HorizonScale — AI Capacity Forecasting Engine
projects.description: Replaced manual forecasting processes with a Python/PySpark forecasting engine and Streamlit dashboard for infrastructure capacity planning. Used telemetry pipelines, Prophet, scikit-learn, and operational reporting patterns to surface future bottlenecks and utilization risks.

projects.name: Serverless Lakehouse Platform — AWS
projects.description: Designed a serverless data-platform pattern using S3, Glue, Athena, Redshift, Bedrock, Parquet, and PySpark concepts. Built learning artifacts around small-file mitigation, columnar storage, ETL design, and Text-to-SQL/RAG-style data access patterns.

projects.name: Databricks Lakehouse Learning Track
projects.description: Self-studied Databricks and lakehouse practices including Delta Lake, medallion architecture, Unity Catalog, DLT, Workflows, schema enforcement, time travel, and merge/upsert patterns, building on production PySpark, Parquet, AWS Glue, and data-platform experience.
